      "それはボードウォーゲームです。",
      "それは4つの別々の戦闘をシミュレートする4つのゲームを含むゲームボックスです。",

    ],

      "それは2人のプレイヤーを対象としています。",
      "それは両側に機械化部隊があります。",
      "それは100個のカウンターを持っています。",
      "それは比較的少ないルールを持っています。",
      "それは2つのシナリオを持っています。",
      "それはキャンペーンゲームを持っています。"

      "それは「多くの雑然とした地形がなく、広大で流動的」であると特徴付けられています。",
      "それは簡単なレベルで砂漠戦争の感覚を与えることを特徴としています。",
      "それは両プレイヤーにとって流動的な動きと戦術的問題を特徴としています。"

IsA: It is a gamebox containing four games simulating four separate battles.
It is a gamebox containing four games that all use the same rules.
It is a gamebox containing four games simulating four separate battles that all use the same rules.
HasA: It has mechanized units on both sides.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized as "simple, easy to play and requires a short period of time (1-2 hours) to complete."
It is characterized by "emphasis on mobile warfare, as the British try to relieve the fortress."
It is characterized by "wide open and fluid without a lot of cluttering terrain."
It is characterized by giving a feel for the desert war at a simple level.
It is characterized by fluid motion and tactical problems for both players.
UsedFor: It is used for simulating Operation Crusader during World War II.
It is used for simulating four separate battles.
It is used for maneuvering by mechanized units.
It is used for attacking an enemy when entering its zone of control.
It is used for attempting to maneuver to surround a unit before combat begins.
It is used for escaping from an impending envelopment.
It is used for a campaign game by linking two scenarios.
It is used for the Axis player to receive victory points for eliminating Allied units.
It is used for the Axis player to receive victory points for cutting supply lines.
It is used for the Allied player to receive victory points for eliminating Axis units.
OriginatedIn: It originated in the Four Battles in North Africa "quadrigame".
CreatedBy: It was created by Frank Davis.
It was created by David C. Isby.
It was created by Redmond A. Simonsen.
PublishedIn: It was published by Simulations Publications Inc. (SPI).
It was published as part of the Four Battles in North Africa "quadrigame".
It was published as an individual "folio game."
ProducedBy: It was produced by Simulations Publications Inc. (SPI).
ConsistsOf: It consists of four games simulating four separate battles.
It consists of mechanized units on both sides.
It consists of relatively few rules.
It consists of a campaign game.
It consists of victory points for the Axis player.
It consists of victory points for the Allied player.
PartOf: It is part of the Four Battles in North Africa "quadrigame".
It is part of a gamebox containing four games.
KnownFor: It is known for simulating Operation Crusader during World War II.
It is known for being part of the Four Battles in North Africa "quadrigame".
It is known for its "emphasis on mobile warfare, as the British try to relieve the fortress."
It is known for being "wide open and fluid without a lot of cluttering terrain."
It is known for giving a feel for the desert war at a simple level.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Operation Crusader.
It is associated with World War II.
It is associated with the Afrika Korps.
It is associated with Erwin Rommel.
It is associated with Allied forces from Egypt.
It is associated with the siege of Tobruk.
It is associated with the "I Go, You Go" alternating turn system.
It is associated with Simulations Publications Inc. (SPI).
It is associated with the Four Battles in North Africa "quadrigame".
It is associated with Frank Davis.
NamedAfter: It is named after Operation Crusader.
It is named after the battle of Tobruk.
About: It is about the battle for Tobruk.
It is about World War II.
It is about the Allied campaign in North Africa.
It is about the siege of Tobruk.
It is about the German counterattack.
It is about the desert war.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by SPI's previous publication The Battle of Borodino.
It was influenced by SPI's first quadrigame, Blue & Gray.
